concept: Interface / Defined Place or Means of System Interaction
description: A defined boundary, connection, or arrangement through which components, systems, or a person and a device interact or exchange matter, energy, or information. An interface may be physical, informational, or procedural. It is not every shared edge or connection, and compatibility, accessibility, safety, and successful exchange must each be established separately.
sound_symbolism: The boundary-bearing 'nora' opens with a firm edge, while relational 'wiso' moves across it in both directions. The four syllables hold separation and contact together without erasing either.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha norawiso lo terulora lorea.' (This interface connects the systems.) State what crosses the interface with ordinary transfer, input, output, or signal clauses. Distinct from 'norui' (boundary), 'lorea' (connect), and a visual control surface alone.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The concept attends to the shaped meeting between distinct forms, where connection depends on how edges are made to relate.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: An interface names the conditions of interaction without assuming that either side understands, accepts, or can use what the other presents.

tags.creation: a designed means through which systems or components interact
tags.dialogue: an informational interface can mediate exchange without guaranteeing understanding
tags.spatial: a boundary or contact location across which exchange occurs
